definition: God's creation of the universe, earth, and all life in six days, resting on the seventh. Establishes God as sovereign Creator and humanity as made in His image.
Adam and Eve's disobedience in eating from the Tree of Knowledge of Good and Evil, resulting in sin entering the world, separation from God, and mortality for all humanity.
God's judgment on the wickedness of humanity by sending a catastrophic flood that covered the entire earth. Only Noah, his family, and the animals on the ark were saved.
The departure of the Israelites from slavery in Egypt, led by Moses. God sent ten plagues upon Egypt and parted the Red Sea to deliver His people.
The night when God's angel of death 'passed over' the homes of Israelites marked with lamb's blood, killing the firstborn of Egypt. It became Israel's most important annual festival.
The foundational moral law given by God to Moses on Mount Sinai, written on two stone tablets. They address humanity's duty to God and to one another.
The execution of Jesus Christ by Roman crucifixion at Golgotha (Calvary) outside Jerusalem. Christians believe this was the atoning sacrifice for the sins of all humanity.
Jesus Christ rising from the dead on the third day after his crucifixion. The cornerstone of Christian faith, it demonstrated victory over sin and death.
The coming of the Holy Spirit upon the apostles fifty days after Jesus' resurrection. They spoke in tongues, and Peter preached, resulting in about 3,000 conversions — the birth of the Church.
Jesus' bodily return to heaven forty days after his resurrection. He was taken up into a cloud while his disciples watched from the Mount of Olives.
The attempt by post-flood humanity to build a tower reaching heaven, demonstrating their defiance of God. God confused their language, causing them to scatter across the earth.
The deportation of the people of Judah to Babylon by King Nebuchadnezzar in 586 BC after the destruction of Jerusalem and the Temple. It lasted approximately 70 years.
An event where Jesus was transformed before Peter, James, and John on a high mountain. His face shone like the sun and his clothes became dazzling white, and Moses and Elijah appeared with him.
Jesus' final meal with his apostles on the night before his crucifixion. He instituted the Lord's Supper (Communion/Eucharist), breaking bread and sharing wine as symbols of his body and blood.
